name: Carbonnade (Belgian Beer Stew)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut beef into 1 x 2 inch strips.
2. Remove ham from bone and cut into cubes. Brown beef and ham in oil in a large skillet. Lift meat out, sprinkle with 1 tsp salt, and set aside.
3. Brown onions in the same oil. Lift onions out, and set aside.
4. Drain and save all but 3 tbsp of the oil.
5. Sift flour into the hot oil to create a light brown roux.
6. Gradually add 1 1/2 cups beef broth, stirring until the mixture boils.
7. Add the remaining beef broth, salt, pepper, sugar, herbs, and garlic. Layer meat, onions, and carrots in a large casserole dish. Add sauce and enough beer to cover the meat. Cover and cook in a 300°F oven for 2 1/2 hours (or on the stove top). Check occasionally and add beer if needed.
8. Shortly before serving, sauté walnuts in the reserved oil until crisp. Do not scorch. Add walnuts to the stew.
9. Just before serving, add vinegar and Scotch whiskey.
